rationale.completeness: Covers all five required areas (accuracy calculation, per-type breakdown, cost tracking, latency metrics, failure categorization) plus rubric parsing and normalization, though some tests are shallow stubs rather than true integration tests.
rationale.correctness: Several tests (TestCostTracking, TestLatencyMetrics, TestPerTypeBreakdown) are not really testing the code under test but rather manually verifying hand-constructed dicts/arithmetic, which gives false confidence; the rate_limit for '429' being categorized as 'tool_error' rather than its own category may or may not match implementation.
rationale.style: Follows pytest class-based organization with descriptive docstrings and clear naming; minor inconsistency in TestRubricParsing using single-quoted JSON in a string that Python's ast.literal_eval might handle but is non-standard for JSON.
rationale.quality: Class-based structure with good docstrings is clean, but several test classes (TestCostTracking, TestLatencyMetrics, TestPerTypeBreakdown) test pure arithmetic or hand-constructed dicts rather than the actual module functions, reducing their value as integration tests.
